calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-15-2010 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-15-2010 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing. Comm: William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Douglas Holloway, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Bank of the West.
Daniel Monberg, Vice President Bank of the West, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendants Jill Seifers or Michael Albertson.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed October 30, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, March 15, 2010.
Plaintiff's Trial Brief was filed today, March 15, 2010.
Default was to defendant Fleet Maintenance Service, LLC was filed on Ocotber 5, 2009.

Exhibit--1--Notice of Time and Place of Trial filed 10/30/09-------------------------EVID/ID.
Exhibit--2--Commercial Lease Agreement dated 12/06/06--------------------------------EVID/ID.
Exhibit--3--Personal Guaranty dated 12/06/06-----------------------------------------EVID/ID.
After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Bank of the Week, and against defendants Jill Seifert, Michael Albertson and Fleet Maintenance Service, LLC as follows:

Principal - $46,309.89.
Prejudgment Interest - $5,387.64.
Attorney Fees - $2,500.00.
Costs - $441.00.

Total - $54,638.53.

Counsel stipulates to the return of the Exhibits.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories And That Req.'S For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Douglas A. Holloway, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST's Motion to order compelling answers to interrogatories and that requests for admissions be deemed admitted; for monetary sanctions granted:
1. All objections are waived, except for attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
2. Defendants Albertson and Seifert shall serve full, complete and verified responses to plaintiff's form interrogatories within fifteen days of notice of entry of this order;
3. The matters set forth in plaintiff's request for admissions are deemed admitted;
4. Sanctions shall issue, jointly and severally against defendants, in the sum of $290.00, payable within thirty days of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-30-2009 Set For Trial On Mar-15-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
